Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Mar-06-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Femi Jordan's Motion to Dismiss (PAGA Action) is GRANTED.

Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted. (See Evidence Code section 452(d).)

Good cause appearing consistent with Code of Civil Procedure 581(b) and Labor Code section 2699 and in light of the judgment in Jordan v. One Medical Group, Inc., Alameda Superior Court Case No. 23CV041507, the court authorizes Plaintiff's dismissal of the instant PAGA action and all claims therein with prejudice.

Plaintiff must file a request for dismissal on the Judicial Council form and may include a copy of this order with that submission.

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order consistent with the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MINI MINUTES:

Matter argued and submitted. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Kelly Shainline, CSR No. 13476, Email: courtreporter232@gmail.com, Tel: 510-828-9404 (302/RBU)

***** After the hearing, the Court signs an order denying defendants' motion to compel arbitration. (See signed order for complete ruling.) (302/RBU) *****

Notice Of Motion To Stay Action Or In The Alternative To Dismiss Plaintiffs Cause Of Action Brought Under The California Private Attorneys General Act
Off Calendar Apr-06-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. HARRY ERGANYAN, Esq. of Lawyers for Justice, PC (Tel: 818-265-1020) for plaintiff;
Atty. AREEN BABAJANIAN, Esq. of Fisher & Phillips LLP (Tel: 213-330-4500) for defendants One Medical Group, Inc., and 1Life Healthcare, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS ONE MEDICAL GROUP, INC. and 1LIFE HEALTHCARE, INC.'s Motion To Stay Action Or In The Alternative To Dismiss Plaintiffs Cause Of Action Brought Under The California Private Attorneys General Act.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' (ONE MEDICAL GROUP, INC., 1LIFE HEALTHCARE, INC.) motion for stay or alternatively, dismissal is denied. Defendants fail to show that a stay is warranted even though the U.S. Supreme Court is currently considering issues that may affect this case in Viking River Cruises, Inc. v. Moriana, No. 20-1573, 2021 WL 5911481, at *1 (U.S. Dec. 15, 2021). Defendants' alternative motion to dismiss is denied. The motion to dismiss is predicated on the contention that representative waivers are enforceable. Such waivers are currently unenforceable in the context of representative PAGA claims, however. (See ZB, N.A. v. Superior Court (2019) 8 Cal.5th 175, 185; Betancourt, v. Prudential Overall Supply (2017) 9 Cal.App. 5th 439, 445-446.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
